title: ideas_review
description: The reviewer's reconciliation of the ideas backlog against shipped work. `reclassifications` are proposed section moves (each backed by concrete evidence); `candidates` are high-value items worth proposing next when the author has writer's block. An empty `reclassifications` list is valid and means the backlog is already current.

reclassifications.description: Proposed moves of an existing ideas.md bullet from one section to another, justified by shipped evidence. Only include a move when there is concrete evidence (a commit, a proposal, a doc) that the item shipped or partially shipped. Leave ambiguous items alone — omit them.

item.description: The bullet text VERBATIM as it appears in ideas.md (without the leading `- `). Used by the reconcile script to find and move the line, so it must match the file.

from.description: Section the item is in today.

to.description: Section it should move to. done ⇒ fully shipped; partial ⇒ partially shipped / in progress.

evidence.description: Concrete proof: a commit subject/sha, a docs/proposals/<file>.md path, or a docs/<file>.md path — what shows this shipped.

confidence.description: How sure the reviewer is that this move is correct.

candidates.description: 2-4 high-value items still in the `## Ideas` section worth proposing next — for an author with writer's block. Ranked best-first.

item.description: The candidate idea, VERBATIM from the `## Ideas` section.

rationale.description: One line: why this is worth doing now (impact, unblocks other work, low cost, etc.).

summary.description: One or two sentences: overall state of the backlog and what the review found.
